title: lupinus missionum
intro: Lupinus missionum, also known as the Mission lupine, is a perennial plant species in the lupine family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Lupinus missionum include its ability to help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce fever, and support respiratory health. Lupinus missionum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its dried leaves and flowers can also be used in smudging ceremonies or as a natural dye.
